name: Global Water Resources, Inc.
business: Global Water Resources, Inc. owns and operates regulated water, wastewater, and recycled water utilities in Arizona, serving over 121,000 people in approximately 40,000 homes. The company generates all revenue from regulated utility services. Its strategy, Total Water Management, integrates water reuse, regional planning, and advanced technology to conserve water and support sustainable growth.

exposures.sentence: GWRI is a water resource management company that owns, operates, and manages thirty-nine water, wastewater, and recycled water public utility systems in strategically located communities, principally in metropolitan Phoenix and Tucson, Arizona.
Our information technology systems may be vulnerable to unauthorized external or internal threats due to hacking, ransomware, viruses or other cybersecurity breaches;
On April 10, 2024, the EPA finalized the NPDWR establishing legally enforceable levels, called MCLs, for six PFAS in drinking water.
Inadequate water supplies and wastewater capacity could have a material adverse effect upon our ability to achieve the customer growth necessary to increase our revenue.

The Company has implemented AMI for approximately 90% of its active customers with a substantial proportion of its remaining customers in the process of being, or planned to be, upgraded with such functionality.

catalysts.sentence: The final 25% increase will be phased in on May 1, 2026.

catalysts.event: Rate case hearing for GW-Santa Cruz and GW-Palo Verde
catalysts.sentence: The hearing with the ALJ, originally scheduled to begin December 15, 2025, was delayed until August 3, 2026, and an additional round of testimony is scheduled for Q2 2026.
